title: 10 best herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain salicin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties similar to aspirin. Salicin helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the joints, which is particularly beneficial for individuals suffering from ankylosing spondylitis, where spinal inflammation can lead to stiffness and rigidity. The cream's topical application allows for targeted relief directly at the site of discomfort, providing fast and effective pain relief without the need for oral medication.
remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in white willow cream have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to relief from symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Tannins: Tannins in white willow cream have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and stiffness in joints and muscles affected by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in salicin, a natural analgesic that helps to reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: The bark contains salicin, which is converted into salicylic acid in the body, providing relief from joint pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried willow bark powder from the salix alba tree.
Mix the willow bark powder with 20g of beeswax and 100ml of sweet almond oil.
Add 10ml of aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 75°c for 30 minutes and then let cool.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized containers and store in a cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams help with ankylosing spondylitis because they possess potent anti-inflammatory compounds that alleviate chronic back pain and stiffness. The active ingredients in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to joint inflammation. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic properties help reduce discomfort and promote relaxation. By applying topical creams infused with herbal ginger extract, individuals with ankylosing spondylitis may experience improved mobility and reduced symptoms. This alternative approach can be a valuable complement to traditional treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the joints, which can alleviate symptoms of AS like pain, stiffness, and swelling.

Analgesic property: The analgesic properties of ginger cream may help relieve pain associated with AS by blocking the production of pain-causing neurotransmitters and reducing prostaglandins, which contribute to pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Ginger's antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the body, which may contribute to the development of AS; antioxidants also help protect against joint degradation and promote overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome, which is the thick underground stem of the ginger plant, contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginger roots and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground ginger powder with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler until melted.
Add vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer or whisk until light and fluffy for 2 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root causes of this debilitating condition. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in joints, which can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, stiffness, and limited mobility. Additionally, turmeric creams may also help to modulate the immune system and prevent further joint damage, promoting improved joint health and function over time. By addressing the underlying inflammatory mechanisms, turmeric creams may provide a natural and effective way to manage ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's active compound curcumin reduces inflammation in the body, which helps alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with AS.
Antioxidant property: Curcumin neutralizes free radicals, which can help prevent or slow down tissue damage and degeneration caused by chronic inflammation in AS patients.
Pain-relieving property: The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of curcumin work together to reduce pain and discomfort experienced by individuals with AS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain high amounts of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are the most commonly used part of the turmeric plant for medicinal purposes, as they contain high amounts of curcuminoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Grind 3 tablespoons of dried turmeric rhizomes into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 2 teaspoons of turmeric powder with 4 teaspoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax and stir until melted, then remove from heat after 2 minutes.
Combine the mixture with 2 tablespoons of distilled water and mix well to form a smooth paste, taking 3 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before refrigerating for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha creams help with ankylosing spondylitis because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively reduce joint pain and stiffness. The cream's active compounds, such as withanolides and alkaloids, work by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby alleviating inflammation and discomfort in the spine and surrounding joints. Additionally, ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help to mitigate stress and anxiety, which are common comorbidities associated with ankylosing spondylitis. By addressing these underlying issues, ashwagandha creams can provide relief and improve overall quality of life for individuals with this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory: Ashwagandha cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and pain in joints, which is a hallmark symptom of Ankylosing Spondylitis.
Pain Relief: The cream's analgesic and anesthetic properties can help alleviate joint pain, stiffness, and discomfort associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis, improving mobility and quality of life.

Immunomodulatory: Ashwagandha's immunomodulatory effects may help regulate the immune system's overactive response, which is believed to contribute to inflammation and damage in Ankylosing Spondylitis, thereby reducing disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain, making them effective in managing symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Rhyzomes: Contain powerful anti-inflammatory agents that target the root cause of joint inflammation and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried ashwagandha root powder and mix it with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f.
Remove from heat and let cool, then filter the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil, mixing until fully incorporated and melted, then pour into sterilized jars.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use, storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively target the underlying inflammation that causes joint pain and stiffness. The cream's active compounds, such as harpagoside, penetrate deep into the skin to reduce swelling and relieve pressure on joints. This relief from inflammation enables improved mobility and flexibility, reducing morning stiffness and joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Additionally, devil's claw creams may help reduce the frequency and severity of flares, providing long-term management and relief for those affected by this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's Claw creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as harpagosides, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: The cream's active ingredients, including harpagide and marutoside, have been shown to possess analgesic and pain-relieving properties, helping to alleviate joint pain and stiffness caused by Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Anti-arthritic effects: Devil's Claw creams have anti-arthritic properties that help reduce joint inflammation and damage associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis, thereby improving joint mobility and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of harpagoside, a bioactive compound that helps to alleviate joint stiffness and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 cups of dried harpagophytum procumbens root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 1 cup of sweet almond oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 160°f.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 20 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into a sterilized glass jar and allow it to cool completely, then store it in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The cream penetrates deep into the skin, targeting areas of chronic inflammation in the joints and spine. By reducing inflammation, St John's wort creams can help to relieve symptoms such as joint stiffness, swelling, and limited mobility. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to slow down or even stop the progression of ankylosing spondylitis, providing long-term relief for those affected by this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: St. John's Wort creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which can reduce inflammation in the joints, muscles, and connective tissues, helping to ease pain and stiffness associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage joint tissue, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall joint health.

Pain-relieving compounds: The essential oil constituents of St. John's Wort, particularly hyperforin, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, providing relief from chronic pain, stiffness, and discomfort associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, the roots of St John's Wort have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and swelling associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of hyperforin, the barks of St John's Wort have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility in cases of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried st. john's wort flowers and 10 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil.
Combine the dried st. john's wort flowers and coconut oil in a glass jar for 14 days of infusion under sunlight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass jar discarding the solids then add 5 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax is melted and fully incorporated into the oil taking approximately 30 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least two hours before transferring to airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can provide relief from chronic pain and stiffness associated with the condition. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can penetrate deep into the skin to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms, allowing for improved flexibility and range of motion. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, which are often exacerbated by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis, allowing for improved joint mobility and reduced stiffness.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic and anodyne properties of lavender help to numb the pain and discomfort caused by ankylosing spondylitis, providing relief from chronic backaches and joint pain.

Relaxation-promoting: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common comorbidities with ankylosing spondylitis, promoting relaxation and improving overall sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Flowers: Contain linalool and linalyl acetate, essential oils that possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for soothing joint discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried lavender flowers from lavandula angustifolia plants and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground lavender powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 10 minutes to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and store it at room temperature for 2 days.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the infused oil, heating the mixture in a double boiler until the wax has melted and the blend reaches 60°c.
Pour the cream into sterile glass jars, allowing it to cool and thicken before use, with a shelf life of 6 months when stored at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively alleviate joint pain, stiffness, and swelling. The menthol in peppermint cream acts as a natural analgesic, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with the condition. Additionally, peppermint's ability to relax muscles and improve circulation may help reduce morning stiffness and increase mobility, making it an effective complementary therapy for managing ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Pain relief: Peppermint's menthol content can help reduce inflammation and numb the affected area, providing temporary pain relief for AS symptoms such as back pain and stiffness.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and discomfort in joints affected by AS, promoting relaxation and reducing joint stiffness.
Digestive support: Peppermint oil has been shown to ease digestive issues common in AS patients, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which can exacerbate symptoms of the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other volatile oils, peppermint roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of peppermint is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making it a valuable addition to creams for treating ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning when oils are most concentrated and dry them immediately.
Combine 20 grams dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters coconut oil in a double boiler and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids then add 10 grams beeswax pellets.
Heat mixture to 60 degrees celsius while stirring until beeswax is fully incorporated and emulsified after 15 minutes.
Pour cream mixture into glass jars and let cool completely at room temperature before storing in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound targets the inflammatory response in the body, which is often the root cause of joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation, feverfew creams can help alleviate symptoms such as morning stiffness, back pain, and limited mobility, allowing individuals to enjoy a greater range of motion and improve their overall quality of life.

Pain-relieving properties: The parthenolide content in feverfew cream has analgesic and anti-pyretic effects, making it effective in reducing joint pain and discomfort caused by ankylosing spondylitis.
Antioxidant properties: Feverfew cream's antioxidant compounds help protect joints from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can contribute to the progression of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to reduce inflammation and pain, making them a suitable choice for creating creams to alleviate joint discomfort associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Flowers: Feverfew flowers contain parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe inflamed joints and muscles in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including 20 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and 100 grams of coconut oil.
Grind the dried tanacetum parthenium flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground tanacetum parthenium powder with 50 grams of beeswax pellets in a double boiler to melt for 15 minutes.
Stir in 100 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of sweet almond oil into the melted mixture until fully incorporated for 10 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they provide a natural way to alleviate the chronic pain, stiffness, and inflammation associated with this condition. The cream's active ingredient, valerenic acid, works by reducing nerve activity in the spinal cord, which in turn decreases pain perception. Additionally, valerian has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and joint inflammation, making it an effective adjunct therapy for managing symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, particularly at night when symptoms often worsen.

Muscle relaxation: Valerian's sedative and relaxant properties can help reduce muscle spasms and stiffness, common symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, allowing for improved flexibility and range of motion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis roots in the fall season for optimal potency.
Grind 250 grams of dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground root powder with 100 milliliters of jojoba oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a clean container and let it cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation. The creams work by penetrating deep into the skin to reach affected joints, where they can alleviate stiffness and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried boswellia serrata resin and organic coconut oil in equal proportions by weight.
Combine the dried boswellia serrata resin and organic coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 5 minutes to ensure even infusion and allow it to cool at room temperature for 4 hours.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding any solids and retaining the infused oil.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture gently in a double boiler until the wax is fully melted and incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it contains compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint swelling and pain. Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ginkgo biloba powder with 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant bowl.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mojave yucca creams helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that target the root causes of the condition. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried yucca schidigera root powder and grind it into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix the ground yucca powder with 200 milliliters of distilled water to create a uniform liquid extract, stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Combine the liquid extract with 50 grams of beeswax pellets, 20 grams of coconut oil, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil in a double boiler, heating for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture continuously until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 65°c to create a smooth, creamy texture, taking approximately 20 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely at room temperature before transferring it to sterilized jars, which can take up to 60 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia frereana resin from mature trees following proper collection guidelines.
Grind the collected boswellia frereana resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 50 grams of ground boswellia frereana with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for two hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves using air circulation at room temperature for 7 days.
Combine 200g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 300ml of olive oil in a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 weeks.

Additionally, calendula's antioxidant properties may also help protect against further damage to affected joints, promoting overall healing and reducing the risk of long-term disability.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with AS.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and exacerbate AS symptoms, promoting overall joint health and reducing oxidative stress.

Immunomodulatory effects: Pot marigold's immunomodulatory compounds may help regulate the immune response, which can contribute to AS inflammation and joint damage by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and increasing anti-inflammatory ones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and tannins, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full bloom and dry them using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 2 hours.
Combine 200g of dried calendula flowers with 400ml of coconut oil in a glass jar and infuse it for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the liquid to a double boiler and heat it at 65°c for 30 minutes.
Add 100g of beeswax and 20g of vitamin e oil to the warmed coconut oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, with a shelf life of 3 years.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis are topical preparations containing natural extracts that provide relief from the chronic pain, stiffness, and inflammation associated with this condition. These creams help by reducing joint inflammation, improving circulation, and soothing muscle tension. Examples of herbal creams effective in managing ankylosing spondylitis include those containing ginger, turmeric, and arnica, which have anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate symptoms and improve mobility. As a result, individuals with ankylosing spondylitis can enjoy improved quality of life, reduced pain and stiffness, and increased flexibility, allowing them to engage in daily activities with greater ease and comfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ankylosing spondylitis with herbal creams is by applying topically formulated blends of natural ingredients such as arnica, ginger, turmeric, and CBD. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help reduce joint stiffness and swelling, promoting comfort and mobility. Regular application can also aid in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, leading to improved overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ankylosing spondylitis that are treated with herbal creams are those characterized by inflammation, stiffness, and pain in the joints. Herbal creams may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and fibromyalgia. These creams typically contain ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, and ginger, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce joint pain and stiffness.
